Notes.Text:
This update for python-jwcrypto fixes the following issues:

- CVE-2026-39373: weak mitigation for JWT bomb attack in the `deserialize` function can lead to memory exhaustion via
  crafted compressed JWE tokens (bsc#1261802).
Notes.Title: Details

Notes.Text: The CVRF data is provided by SUSE under the Creative Commons License 4.0 with Attribution (CC-BY-4.0).
Notes.Title: Terms of Use

Relationships.RelatesToProductReference: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server for SAP applications 16.0

Vulnerabilities.Description: JWCrypto implements JWK, JWS, and JWE specifications using python-cryptography. Prior to 1.5.7, an unauthenticated attacker can exhaust server memory by sending crafted JWE tokens with ZIP compression. The existing patch for CVE-2024-28102  limits input token size to 250KB but does not validate the decompressed output size. An unauthenticated attacker can cause memory exhaustion on memory-constrained systems. A token under the 250KB input limit can decompress to approximately 100MB. This vulnerability is fixed in 1.5.7.

ProductID: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server for SAP applications 16.0:python313-jwcrypto-1.5.6-160000.3.1
